segments.text: dont need fixes, but currently the sync status on some .murder/plans reads conflic and parse_error. mind looking into that for me and letting me know what the deal is?

segments.input: sqlite3 murder.db "SELECT name, status, sync_state, вҖҰ FROM plans"

segments.text: Let me confirm the valid statuses and what's actually in those files.

segments.title: head -8 each of plan-jk-crash / plan-tui-polish-cleanup / plan-done-crow-cleanup

segments.text:
Got it вҖ” here's the deal. Three plans are off synced, and there are two distinct causes.

parse_error (2 plans) вҖ” invalid status: in frontmatter

The PlanStatus enum (murder/work/plans/schema.py:25) only accepts three values: draft, accepted, superseded. These two files have a status: that isn't one of them, so parse() throws and reconcile_file parks the plan in parse_error (sync.py:189-201):

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ            File            в”Ӯ status: value в”Ӯ Valid? в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ plan-jk-crash.md           в”Ӯ open          в”Ӯ вң—      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ plan-tui-polish-cleanup.md в”Ӯ active        в”Ӯ вң—      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

Both look like hand-edits. The DB still has the last good copy of each (both draft); the markdown edits just aren't being ingested while the status is unparseable. A one-word edit in each file clears it on the next poll.

conflict (1 plan) вҖ” both sides changed

plan-done-crow-cleanup hit the db_changed and file_changed branch (sync.py:223-231): both the DB body and the markdown diverged from the last materialized hash. Sync won't guess a winner, so it parks it as conflict.

Net: the two parse_errors are benign frontmatter mismatches; the one conflict is the sync loop correctly refusing to auto-merge a genuine two-sided divergence. Happy to fix any/all if you want, but leaving as-is per your ask.
wondering if we arrived at this state becasue of changes to murder codebase and these are old files, or is the current pipeline capable of leading to wedged state like this?
Good question вҖ” let me distinguish "stale artifacts" from "reachable today," and check whether the current writer could ever produce these.

segments.title: git log -L PlanStatus schema.py В· file histories В· parser.py В· frontmatter

segments.text:
Traced it end to end. Short answer: the two parse_errors are bad-data-origin (never producible by the pipeline itself), but the one conflict is fully reachable by the current pipeline вҖ” and it's a sticky terminal state.

parse_error вҶ’ external/hand edits, not a schema migration

The writer can't produce these. render() always emits status: plan.status.value, a valid enum member by construction. PlanStatus has been exactly draft/accepted/superseded since the initial scaffold (2e8fbf3); open and active were never valid here. plan-jk-crash.md uses a foreign frontmatter schema (title:/created:) entirely; plan-tui-polish-cleanup.md is canonical-format but had its status hand-edited to active. So: not a codebase-change artifact вҖ” but reconcile_file will re-raise parse_error any time someone edits frontmatter to an unsupported status. Reachable only via out-of-band edits; arguably working as intended.

conflict вҶ’ reachable today, and won't self-heal

This is the live concern. The branch at sync.py:223-231 fires whenever both DB body and markdown diverged from last_materialized_hash вҖ” a plain two-writer race (agent writes a revision while the file is edited out-of-band). With a 1.5s poll / 0.75s debounce, that window is real.

Worse, it's sticky. mark_plan_sync_state updates only file_hash, never last_materialized_hash/body_hash (plans.py:251-259), so once conflicted it re-evaluates to conflict indefinitely; even re-editing the file doesn't escape. No service-layer "resolve conflict" affordance exists вҖ” only rename_plan/deprecate_plan force synced.

Bottom line: parse_error = stale/foreign input correctly rejected; conflict = a genuine, current-pipeline-reachable wedge with no built-in resolver. The cheap fix is giving conflict an escape hatch (a "keep file" / "keep DB" action that resets last_materialized_hash).

have an opus subagent compare the two document versions and fix the current state so we don't lose anything. You and I will keep chatting about this. How do products like google drive and similar solve this type of problem? are there patterns worth using in this codebase? I could bandaid the problem by similarly having a planning agent call to resolve, but that's not a real fix.
I'll dispatch an Opus subagent to safely reconcile the conflicted plan in the background, then we can talk through the design while it works.

Subagent's reconciling plan-done-crow-cleanup in the background (with backups of both versions first, so it's loss-proof even if its merge is imperfect). Now to your real question.

What kind of problem this actually is

You've built bidirectional replication between two replicas (a SQLite row and a markdown file) reconciled by polling snapshots. The defining limitation is in last_materialized_hash: you compare hashes of current state, not streams of edits. Snapshots detect divergence but give you nothing to merge with. Every product below is a different answer to "what do you retain so merge is possible, and at what granularity?"

How the big products solve it

Dropbox / Google Drive (opaque files): detect, never merge, never lose вҖ” keep one canonical and write the other to (conflicted copy).md. Crude, but never lossy and never sticky; your conflict state is strictly worse.

Google Docs (OT): a central server serializes every keystroke as transformable ops. Needs an always-on mediator and edits-as-operations. Your "poll the file every 1.5s" is the opposite. Not applicable.

Notion/Linear/Figma (CRDTs): converge with no coordinator, but the doc stops being a markdown file / TEXT column and becomes a CRDT structure. Enormous overkill for one agent + one occasional human editor.

git (diff3): three-way merge against a common ancestor вҖ” this is the one that fits, because git solves exactly your shape: two divergent snapshots plus a known merge base. Non-overlapping edits auto-merge; only genuine overlaps conflict.

What I'd actually do (not a bandaid)

murder already stores almost everything a 3-way merge needs: last_materialized_hash is your merge-base pointer, and plan_revisions retains historical bodies + hashes. You're storing the ancestor; you just aren't using it. So, cheapest first:

1. Stop the wedge (do regardless) вҖ” conflict must have an exit; advance the hashes so re-editing escapes. The bug-shaped part.
2. 3-way merge on reconcile (the real fix) вҖ” recover the base from plan_revisions, run diff3 of base/db/file; clean merges go straight to synced.
3. Conflict-copy fallback for true overlaps (Dropbox's trick) вҖ” write conflict markers or spill to <name>.conflict-<ts>.md; loss-proof, never sticky. This is where an agent-resolve belongs вҖ” not as detector/merger (that's deterministic diff3), only for residual overlaps.
4. Collapse the dual-write вҖ” make the file canonical, DB a rebuildable read-model; deletes the whole conflict class. Biggest change, most architecturally honest.

Recommendation: 2 + 3 now (reuse existing assets, match git semantics), 1 as stop-the-bleeding, 4 filed for when plan editing gets reworked anyway.

The subagent finished cleanly вҖ” plan-done-crow-cleanup is back to synced, nothing lost, and it held across a full poll cycle. It did the file-write + DB-update in one transaction and re-verified after a poll вҖ” itself a small proof the convergence approach is sound.

What it merged: body = file side (a strict superset of the DB body except one stale "failed agents are out of scope" bullet the new ## Scope section reverses вҖ” dropping it lost no info); related_tickets = union [t007, t009]; truthful frontmatter (revisions=1), status=draft; backups at /tmp/plan-done-crow-cleanup.file.bak and .db.bak. Final hashes all converged; conflict_reason and parse_error cleared.

For the design discussion: the agent effectively performed a manual 3-way merge but had to reconstruct the DB side via render() because the base wasn't handed to it. The single overlap it resolved is precisely the residual diff3 would surface вҖ” everything else would've auto-merged. Live confirmation that 2 (diff3) + 3 (escalate the true overlap) is the right shape: deterministic merge for the 90%, judgment only for the genuine semantic conflict.

Want me to sketch the diff3 reconcile path concretely against sync.py, including pulling the base body out of plan_revisions? I can keep it to a design sketch (no edits) since you said no fixes yet.
